title: Void or supersede erroneous transactions
description: Correct erroneous canonical transaction facts through attributable void or supersession history instead of edit or deletion. Implements G2 and UC-LEDGER-009.
rationale: Original source facts and the reason for correction must remain auditable while active actuals include only the valid outcome.
story_want: correct an erroneous transaction without erasing history
story_so_that: actuals become correct and the original fact remains explainable
acceptance_criteria: Given an active transaction and a reason, when the owner voids it, then it becomes inactive, the void event is attributable, and default actuals exclude it.

Given an active transaction, a reason, and valid replacement facts, when the owner supersedes it, then one replacement is created, the original becomes inactive, and default actuals include only the replacement.
Given a transaction involved in an active transfer or refund relationship, when it is voided or superseded, then the relationship is retired atomically with the correction.

Given a voided or superseded transaction, when history-inclusive detail is requested, then the original facts, correction reason, actor context, time, and replacement reference where applicable remain queryable.
